commit.message:
Preserve hatches in geometry legend proxies (#612)

* Preserve geometry hatch styles in legend proxies

Cartopy geometry artists created with add_geometries() were keeping hatch on the plotted FeatureArtist but dropping it when UltraPlot built the PathPatch legend proxy. This updates the geometry legend handler to copy common patch-style properties more generically, including hatch, while keeping the existing joinstyle fallback.

A regression test now covers add_geometries(..., hatch='/', label=...) so semantic geometry legends preserve the plotted hatch pattern.

Closes #611

* Generalize geometry legend patch style copying

* Document geometry legend style-copy rationale

* Black

* Document geometry legend proxy limitations

* Update ultraplot/legend.py

Refactor/dotfiles optimization (#7)

* refactor: optimize dotfiles structure and fix bugs

- Fix code quality issues: duplicate code, syntax errors, security issues
- Standardize code style: use #!/usr/bin/env bash, add set -euo pipefail

* feat(bootstrap): add smart installation modes

Add new command-line options for flexible installation:
- -c, --config-only: Only run setup, skip tool installation
- -u, --update: Force tool installation/update
- -h, --help: Show help message

Smart behavior:
- First run (no ~/.dotfiles_initialized): setup + tool installation
- Subsequent runs: only setup by default
- --update flag forces tool installation on any run

Fix: Use global variables instead of command substitution
to allow proper exit on --help flag.

This allows users to quickly update dotfiles config without
reinstalling all tools, while still supporting full updates
when needed.

commit.message:
ci: add AFL++ iccFromCube job to LibFuzzer workflows

Add afl-fromcube job to both libfuzzer-smoke-test.yml and
cfl-libfuzzer-parallel.yml. Each job:
- Installs afl++ from Ubuntu 24.04 repos (4.09c)
- Clones and builds iccDEV with afl-clang-fast++ (ASAN+UBSAN)
- Runs AFL with seed corpus and dictionary from cfl/
- Uploads crash/hang artifacts
- Reports stats in job summary

Smoke test runs 60s, parallel uses configurable fuzz_seconds.

commit.message:
Fix differentiating GPU Dense layer (#910) (#1044)

* Fix differentiating GPU Dense layer (#910)

Flux's Dense layer on CUDA failed because Mooncake traced into CUBLAS
internals and into NNlib's GPU broadcast fallback, both of which contain
types with no tangent_type/fdata_type methods.

Two primitive rules are added to intercept before the problematic internals:

1. ext/MooncakeCUDAExt.jl — LinearAlgebra.mul!(C, A, B) on CuArrays
   Add @is_primitive + frule!! + rrule!! for 3-arg mul!(C, A, B) on
   CuMaybeComplexArray, covering matrix×matrix and matrix×vector for
   real and complex IEEEFloat. Also uses arrayify in the sum frule!!
   instead of primal/tangent directly.

2. ext/MooncakeNNlibExt.jl — NNlib.bias_act!(identity, x, b) on GPU arrays
   NNlib's ChainRules rrule for bias_act! requires a RuleConfig, which
   @from_rrule cannot supply. On AbstractGPUArray, bias_act! falls back
   to non-in-place x .+ b, so the rule returns a new output array with
   its own gradient buffer and accumulates gradients for x and b.

Tests: mul! cases added to test/ext/cuda/cuda.jl; bias_act! cases added
to test/ext/nnlib/nnlib.jl (covers both CPU and GPU via _rand).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix bias_act! GPU rule to be in-place

With CUDA ≥ 5.9.7, NNlib's bias_act!(identity, x, b) modifies x in-place
(x .+= b) rather than returning a new array. The old frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } and rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             }
allocated a fresh output array, which caused the primal check
has_equal_data(x_primal, map(primal, x_x̄_rule)) to fail.

Rewrite both rules following the same in-place pattern used by the mul!
rule in MooncakeCUDAExt:
  and in the pullback restore px from the copy after accumulating gradients.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(cuda): replace TODO comment with cross-reference

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix bias_act! rule to cover CPU arrays too

The @is_primitive / frule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } / rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             } were registered only for
AbstractGPUArray, but the test asserts is_primitive=true for both CPU
and GPU arrays.  Switch from the local GPUIEEEArray alias to the
existing SupportedArray{<:IEEEFloat} (= Union{Array, AbstractGPUArray}),
which covers both.  Also fix the now-stale test comment.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update MooncakeNNlibExt.jl

Signed-off-by: Hong Ge <3279477+yebai@users.noreply.github.com>

* Change stability parameter in CUDA tests

Signed-off-by: Astitva Aggarwal <84859349+AstitvaAggarwal@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update nnlib.jl

Signed-off-by: Astitva Aggarwal <84859349+AstitvaAggarwal@users.noreply.github.com>

* format

* CuPtr fdata_type, rdata_type

* Fix SupportedArray{P,N} → SupportedArray{P} in bias_act! rrule            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             }

SupportedArray is a type alias with one type parameter {P}, so
SupportedArray{P,N} caused a 'too many parameters for type' error that
prevented MooncakeNNlibExt from compiling. Replace the N type parameter
with ndims(px) computed at runtime.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix type parameter for SupportedArrays & usage

product-led SaaS landing page inspired by Stripe, Vercel, Linear,
and Ramp.

Changes:
- Remove Tailwind CDN and Lucide CDN dependencies
- New styles.css: custom modular CSS with variables, responsive grid,
  glassmorphism, animations, and hover effects
- New script.js: nav scroll, mobile menu toggle, scroll reveal
- index.html: full rewrite with 7 sections:
  • Sticky nav with mobile hamburger menu
  • Hero with gradient mesh bg, inline SVG product mockup (floating
    glassmorphic cards showing platform stats), trust badges
  • Product overview: Hadhiya Fihaara with 4 feature cards
  • Platform capabilities: 4-item grid (shield, scale, mobile, uptime)
  • About Ignite Labs: two-column with stat cards
  • CTA section with gradient accent and orange top rule
  • Footer: 3-column with logo, nav links, contact email

Design system:
  • Color: #06070f base, #f35d28 brand orange
  • Font: Plus Jakarta Sans (Google Fonts)
  • Animations: float keyframes on mockup cards, live-pulse dot,
    IntersectionObserver scroll reveal with staggered delays
  • Fully responsive: desktop/tablet/mobile breakpoints
